summary.intent: Update the Autocomplete spec for task #255 to reflect a framework-agnostic selection controller contract, then implement the core Rust module following a TDD + post-implementation audit workflow, and ship a non-draft GitHub PR.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented `crates/ars-components/src/selection/autocomplete/mod.rs` with full state machine, all focused test gates green (unit, spec-conformance, proptest, snapshot, doctest), mutation score at 0 missed out of 149 tested, 93.15% line coverage, and a non-draft PR pushed to GitHub with commit closing issue #255.

repo: `cargo xci --profile fast` is the required pre-push CI gate; it runs fmt, check, clippy, unit tests, snapshot count, and spec validation sequentially.
`cargo xtask spec validate` enforces frontmatter contracts including a `related` field; omitting or mismatching that field is a hard validation failure.

`cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` is unusable as a scoped filter gate because insta counts filtered-out snapshots as unreferenced; use `cargo insta test -p ars-components -- selection::autocomplete` instead.
`cargo mutants` runs the full test baseline by default and hits unrelated failures; pass `-- <filter>` to scope baseline runs to the new module's tests.
`cargo mutants --iterate` reuses prior caught/unviable results but invalidates the skip set when source files change (e.g., after `cargo fmt`), triggering a full re-pass.
Session recovery artifacts live in `.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl`; prior state is recoverable via `jq` queries on those files.

The repo has a mandatory post-implementation audit skill (`.agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md`) covering spec-to-code drift, adapter spec alignment, catalog entries, and coverage gating.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs (`spec/leptos-components/`, `spec/dioxus-components/`) must be updated in sync with core spec changes even when no adapter code is written.
The component catalog at `spec/foundation/02-component-catalog.md` contains prose summaries that drift from implementation and must be patched as part of any spec update.

code.finding: New file: agnostic Autocomplete state machine owning input value, visible_keys, highlighted_key, loading, debounce_pending, and selected_key; delegates DOM focus, positioning, scrolling, and async fetch to adapters.
Added `pub mod autocomplete;` export to surface the new module.
Added anatomy coverage asserting all Autocomplete part roles and ARIA attributes against the updated spec.
Added property-test invariants for highlight/visible-key consistency across arbitrary event sequences.

Expanded from Root/Input/ClearTrigger stub to full owned anatomy: Content, Item, ItemText, EmptyState, LoadingIndicator, LiveRegion; states Idle/Focused/Interacting/Loading; debounce and loading props.
Stale catalog entry described Autocomplete as a SearchInput/Menu pairing with role=menu; updated to reflect owned listbox-suggestion contract.

workflow: TDD red-green flow: add failing tests that fail at import/module resolution first, confirm failure, then write the implementation — proves the public surface didn't exist before the code landed.
Mutation testing should be scoped with `-- <test-filter>` from the start to avoid unrelated baseline failures; add `#[mutants::skip]` or behavioral assertions iteratively until 0 missed.
`cargo llvm-cov report --summary-only | rg '<module>|TOTAL'` is the fastest way to check per-file line coverage after an instrumented run.
Post-implementation audit should check adapter specs and the component catalog as named deliverables, not just the core spec and implementation files.
When `--iterate` invalidates due to source changes, treat it as a full re-pass rather than trying to preserve prior results; the overhead is comparable to the gap-filling tests added.

friction: Session recovery required manually grepping `.codex/sessions/` JSONL files and the worktree was detached at `origin/main`, adding setup overhead before any implementation work.
`cargo xtask spec digest autocomplete` and `cargo xtask spec toc` hung on Cargo's package-cache lock during initial exploration, requiring fallback to direct Markdown reads.
Broad `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` hit pre-existing data-display snapshot failures unrelated to the Autocomplete work, blocking its use as a clean gate.
Three full mutation runs plus one `--iterate` pass were needed to drive missed mutants to zero; each full run took significant wall-clock time due to 149 mutants × compile+test cycle.
`cargo mutants --iterate` invalidated the prior skip set after `cargo fmt` ran, requiring a fourth full pass instead of a cheap re-check.
`cargo xci --profile fast` took noticeably longer than its nominal runtime due to broad dependency recompilation in a fresh worktree.
Spec validation failed on `related` frontmatter mismatch, requiring a targeted revert after the initial spec patch.
Multiple clippy lint fixes were needed in the new module after the implementation pass, requiring an extra edit-compile-fix cycle.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter wrapper components (`ars-leptos`, `ars-dioxus`) were not implemented — only their specs were updated; adapter implementations remain as future work.
Package-wide `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` is broken due to pre-existing unrelated snapshot hygiene issues; needs a separate cleanup pass across the repo.
6.85% of lines in `autocomplete/mod.rs` are uncovered (93.15% line coverage); specific uncovered paths were not identified and may warrant additional edge-case tests.
Browser/E2E tests for the Autocomplete component are not yet possible since no adapter rendering layer was implemented in this task.
